message:
fwc: complete TODO security scaffolding in generated connector tests

br-flywheel_connectors-r3jq9

Scaffolded connectors emitted 4 unit tests and 2 e2e tests with
empty bodies marked only with TODO comments where security checks
should live, so every `fwc new` output shipped with placeholder
default-deny / error-taxonomy / redaction coverage. Replace the
placeholders with real test bodies that exercise the invariants
the comments promised:

* test_missing_capability_denied: build a capability token whose
  `operations` slice is empty, sign it with the correct key, assert
  invoke returns an FCP-3xxx/FCP-4xxx error (cap/zone class). This
  exercises the capability-matching path rather than signature
  verification — the default-deny security model.

* test_network_constraints_enforced: parse the generated fcp.toml
  via include_str!, assert it declares no host_allow entries, keeps
  system.exec/system.privileged in the forbidden list, and sets a
  strict sandbox profile. Freezes the scaffold's own declaration so
  future widening requires an intentional change.

* test_secrets_not_logged: render the connector's Config via Debug,
  assert the output contains none of the common secret-named field
  markers (api_key, client_secret, refresh_token, access_token,
  password, bearer, private_key). Forward-looking regression gate:
  when a future author adds a secret-named field without
  `#[serde(skip)]` or a redacting Debug impl, this test fails.

* test_error_codes_correct: strengthen the already-partial body to
  assert error_code() matches the FCP-NNNN shape AND that unknown
  operations specifically surface as FcpError::InvalidRequest. The
  prior TODO mis-described the expected range (FCP-5xxx); unknown
  operations actually fall in the protocol-error range (FCP-1xxx)
  per fcp_core::FcpError::numeric_code.

* test_e2e_decision_receipt_shape / test_e2e_audit_event_shape:
  kept `#[ignore]` because they require integration harnesses, but
  flesh out the expected field-shape assertions as a commented
  template so the test is ready the moment the host-side capture
  side-channel is available.

Also fix the scaffolded manifest's capability declaration: the
generated operation's capability (`{short}.scaffold_status`) must
appear in `capabilities.required` per manifest lint. Previously
`required = []` while the operation declared `capability =
"<short>.scaffold_status"` caused the lint to fail downstream when
the scaffold was actually installed; it also blocked the
`emit_truthful_*_fixture_bundle` golden-regen tests from running.

All six archetype golden bundles regenerated via
`FCP_UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 cargo test --ignored emit_truthful`; the
six `*_scaffold_matches_golden_bundle` tests pass against the new
bundles. The pre-existing
`generate_unit_tests_rs_has_test_scaffolds` regression continues
to pass (the scaffolded function names are unchanged).

Files: crates/fwc/src/new_cmd.rs (+201 -30),
       crates/fwc/testdata/generator/truthful_{archetype}_bundle.json (6 goldens).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
